title: Vorkath/Strategies
wikitext:
[[File:Fighting Vorkath.png|thumb|300px|A player fighting Vorkath, a powerful blue dragon enhanced by Zorgoth's experiments.]] After completion of [[Dragon Slayer II]], [[Vorkath]] can be fought again. This time, it is stronger. To get to [[Vorkath]], talk to [[Torfinn]], located in [[Rellekka]] on the 3rd dock to the right. This brings you to [[Ungael]], at the entrance of [[Vorkath]]. '''{{Colour|red|Warning}}:''' Beware of Vorkath's acid pool quickfire barrage, insta-kill vertical dragonfire, and suicide spawn as each of these attacks can kill players almost immediately. ==Requirements== * Completion of {{SCP|Quest}}[[Dragon Slayer II]] for access to [[Ungael]]. ==Suggested skills== *{{SCP|Ranged|85+}} (Ranged method) *{{SCP|Attack|80+}} (Melee method) *{{SCP|Strength|85+}} (Melee method) *{{SCP|Defence|75+}} *{{SCP|Prayer|70+}} for [[Piety]] (74+ for [[Rigour]]) *{{SCP|Construction|82+}} (for [[Ornate rejuvenation pool]] (with boost) - (optional for faster resupply and resetting)) ==Fight overview== [[Vorkath]] is similar to most dragons in that it is weak against stab melee and ranged. Upon awakening, Vorkath's attack pattern consists of six random standard attacks, one special attack, another six standard attacks, then the other special attack, and so on until defeated. During the fight, it is recommended to always maintain at least a [[super antifire potion]] and [[Protect from Magic]], or a regular [[antifire potion]] and a dragonfire protection shield. Whether to have run on during the fight is a matter of preference. Keeping run on, except for during the acid special attack, increases the margin for error when dodging the fireball attack, but requires the player to manually toggle run when the acid attack occurs. Leaving run off eliminates the need to toggle and players can rely on quick reactions to dodge the fireball while walking, and use Ctrl-click to override the run setting if needed. The optimal [[protection prayer]]s to use will depend on the gear being used: *[[Protect from Magic]] if using [[melee]], a [[toxic blowpipe]], or if holding a [[book of law]] (along with a super antifire potion) *[[Protect from Missiles]] if using a crossbow with a [[dragonfire ward]], [[dragonfire shield]] or [[anti-dragon shield]] Vorkath is capable of [[Venom|envenoming]] players with its venom dragonfire; as such, bring appropriate venom protection. The prayer dragonfire turns all active prayers off, including [[Preserve]]; for this reason, divine potions are recommended over normal potions. Additionally, recoil damage such as [[Vengeance]] and [[Ring of recoil|rings of recoil]] do not work on Vorkath. ===Standard attacks=== Vorkath uses the following standard attacks: [[File:Vorkath - deadly dragonfire.mp4|thumb|400px|Vorkath's deadly dragonfire attack directly striking a player.]] {| class="wikitable" ! Image !! Name !! Notes |- | |[[File:Vorkath.png|150px]] || Melee Attack || Deals [[melee]] damage up to 32. |- | |[[File:Vorkath magic attack.png|150px]] || Magic Attack || Deals [[magic]] damage up to 30. Used more frequently if in melee distance. |- | |[[File:Vorkath ranged attack.png|150px]]|| Ranged Attack|| Deals [[ranged]] damage up to 32. Used more frequently if out of melee distance.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/966003811084111872?s=19|date=20 February 2018|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/103777|archivedate=29 May 2020|quote=Relative weightings of 3 for magic, 4 for ranged.}} |} * Vorkath's standard attacks are '''unreactable''', and therefore the player should constantly pray against the attack style they are weakest to *[[Dragonfire]]: Aside from standard dragonfire, Vorkath has multiple variants that it can use, similar to the [[King Black Dragon]] ===Standard Dragonfire=== {{:Dragonfire/Vorkath}} ===Advanced dragonfire=== Vorkath's advanced dragonfire attacks that are not all affected by dragonfire protection. {| class="wikitable" ! Image !! Name !! Notes |- | |[[File:Vorkath dragonfire attack.png|150px]]|| Standard Dragonfire|| Deals [[dragonfire]] damage dependent on the number of layers of dragonfire protection used by the player. Full damage mitigation requires a [[super antifire potion]] along with a dragonfire protection shield. Players also typically use a regular antifire potion with a shield that protects against dragonfire, or a super antifire potion along with the protect from magic prayer; both combinations reduce the max hits of dragonfire to 10. |- | |[[File:Vorkath venom dragonfire attack.png|150px]]|| Venomous Dragonfire || Inflicts [[venom]] if not immune. Dragonfire protection does negate damage inflicted by this attack, but does not protect against the infliction of venom. |- | |[[File:Vorkath prayer disabling attack.png|150px]]|| Corrupting Dragonfire || Deactivates all [[prayers]] upon impact. Players should have quick-prayers accordingly set to reactivate their prayers immediately afterward. Dragonfire protection does negate damage inflicted by this attack, but does not protect against the prayer disabling effect. |- | |[[File:Vorkath deadly dragonfire attack.png|150px]]|| Deadly Dragonfire || Vertically launched at the player's position when fired. This will often outright kill the player if hit; if adjacent to the strike zone, it will deal half the damage it would have dealt. Thus, players should always be prepared to click two tiles away from where they are at all times. This attack cannot be defended against with any type of dragonfire protection. |} ===Rapid Fire=== [[File:Vorkath acid pool attack.png|left|150px]] Rapid Fire is the deadliest of Vorkath's attacks, and is one of the two special attacks it'll use after six standard attacks. When used, Vorkath will launch acid pools around the arena (with one landing on where the player stood), then fire dragonbreath every tick at the tile the player is standing on. Standing on or running over an acid pool will deal up to 10 damage, which Vorkath will [[life leech|leech]] as health. Getting hit by the rapid-fire dragonfire will deal 25-41 damage regardless of the player's dragonfire protection. As such, the basic principle to surviving this special attack is to always be on the move whilst '''walking''' on clean tiles. Players can find a line (at least five tiles free of acid) and walk back and forth said line. This is relatively safe but no damage will be dealt to Vorkath in the process. Players new to fighting Vorkath may use the fact that there is always an acid-free line directly next to the exit, when walking side to side ('hugging' the wall). Be wary that running to this back line while the acid is being thrown has a chance of spawning an acid pool in this line, because theres a chance Vorkath throws acid right behind you, regardless of proximity to the entrance. ====The Woox Walk==== Experienced players may utilise a technique known as the "Woox Walk", named after the player who devised it, to continue attacking Vorkath whilst avoiding his dragonfire attacks. Mastering the Woox Walk is highly recommended for players seeking to kill Vorkath many times over, as this significantly decreases kill time and results in more kills per hour, despite the halved damage he takes during this special attack. It is generally easier to learn with sounds enabled, as you should click every time you hear Vorkath shoot its fireball. Practicing the moves throughout the fight is also useful for learning with no punishment upon mistakes. The basic principle of the Woox Walk involves tick-perfect movements to move towards Vorkath to attack, then immediately moving to another tile out of the player's attack range, then moving to attack again once they are able. It is recommended to enable tile highlighting in the [[settings]], then enabling "Highlight current tile", as this helps visualise which tile you are on rather than using your character to do so. The way in which the Woox Walk is performed is dependent on the type of weapon chosen: Vorkath - Woox Walk (lance).mp4|If using melee, as soon as your attack animation starts, immediately walk two spaces back and attack Vorkath again. This is best done with a 4-tick weapon such as the [[dragon hunter lance]]. Vorkath - Woox Walk (blowpipe).mp4|If using a [[toxic blowpipe]], the yellow tile highlighted indicates the first row of tiles in which the player is out of attack range. Thus, clicking to attack Vorkath will force you to move one tile forward; move back between two clean tiles to stay on the attack. The blowpipe's special attack will heal the damage the player would have dealt rather than half. Vorkath - Woox Walk (crossbow).mp4|If using a [[Crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]] with an [[attack range]] of 7, the row of tiles along the perimeter of the arena is the only location where the player is out of range. Players must move along this perimeter, then attack when in front of a clean tile. Weapons with an attack range of 8 or greater (notably the Armadyl and Zaryte crossbows) will always be in range and are not viable for this tactic. ===Zombified Spawn=== [[File:Vorkath zombified ice attack.png|left|150px]] [[File:Vorkath - Zombified Spawn.mp4|thumb|400px|A player deals with Vorkath's Zombified Spawn. Despite the delayed attack, the spawn will not deal damage as long as they are killed before they reach the player, even if it appears to as shown.]] Vorkath's other special attack involves launching an icy breath toward the player. While this deals no damage, it will freeze the player in place, and Vorkath will spit out a [[Zombified Spawn]] that always lands ten tiles away from the player. Vorkath is immune to damage as soon as the breath is launched, and remains as such until the spawn is killed or explodes. The spawn has 38 Hitpoints and will walk towards the player. If it reaches the player, it will explode, dealing damage based on its current Hitpoints prior to explosion; this can deal up to 60 damage. Casting [[Crumble Undead]] will instantly kill the spawn, and it will always hit, provided that the player's Magic attack bonus is above -64. If the spell is cast and lands on the spawn just as it reaches the player, it will explode, but no damage will be incurred. Once the spawn is killed or explodes, the freeze will wear off and Vorkath can be damaged again. It is best to bring a magic staff swap, ideally a [[slayer's staff]] or [[dust battlestaff]] for the spawn; the former allows for auto-casting, while the latter eliminates the need for [[dust rune]]s. It is important to avoid attacking after Vorkath's six auto-attack, since Vorkath becomes immune to damage before the breath is launched and also prevents delay from setting in. While there is more than enough time to kill the spawn if this occurs, Vorkath is immune to damage, rendering the extra attacks null. One way to avoid continuing to attack Vorkath is to keep auto-retaliate off and eat food or drink a potion when hit with the icy breath. This will stop the player from attacking Vorkath and give more time to cast Crumble Undead on the spawn. ==Vorkath trip efficiency== ===Divine potions=== It is recommended to use divine potions over normal potions at Vorkath, as the prayer-disabling dragonfire's timing often causes [[Preserve]]'s +30 second timer duration to be nulled, especially if it hits when the stat drain tick falls below 15. ===Defence reduction=== Players, especially those using a [[toxic blowpipe]], should attempt to lower Vorkath's [[Defence]] with the [[Bandos godsword]] or [[dragon warhammer]] [[special attack]]s at the start of each kill. At lower ranged levels or if struggling with the fight, failing to hit at least one dragon warhammer special attack or not dealing enough damage with the Bandos godsword special may cause the fight to last much longer. If this is the case, resetting and replenishing your special attack energy at the [[player-owned house]] may be better for getting more consistent killcount. If using [[Dragonbane weapons|dragonbane weaponry]] or [[Osmumten's fang]], defence reducing special attacks can be substituted for more damaging special attacks, such as the [[burning claws]], [[Voidwaker]], or [[Zaryte crossbow]]. ===Banking and transportation=== It is recommended to use [[Fremennik sea boots 4]]; have your [[house]] in [[Rellekka]] (or have the [[Construction cape]]); use an [[Enchanted lyre]] (or [[Enchanted lyre(i)]]); use a [[Rellekka teleport]]; or have a portal attuned to [[Lunar Isle]] or [[Waterbirth Island]]. After each successful [[Vorkath]] attempt using the [[toxic blowpipe]], or once out of supplies with the [[dragon hunter crossbow]], players should re-gear with one of the following methods: *'''POH without pool''': On world 330 (or 465/512), set your POH to [[Rimmington]] or [[Yanille]], teleport to outside your [[player-owned house]] with a [[Teleport to house|house tablet]], runes in your [[rune pouch]] or [[max cape]]/[[Construct. cape|construction cape]], enter a host's house at that location and use their [[ornate rejuvenation pool]]. This method requires a way of returning to [[Vorkath]] that doesn't depend on a [[Rellekka]] POH teleport. You can also use the [[Miscellania]] teleport from an [[Ornate jewellery box|Ornate Jewellery Box]], run to the port, then use the [[Sailor]] to travel to [[Rellekka]] *'''POH with pool''': Teleport to your [[player-owned house]] with a [[Teleport to house|house tablet]], runes in your [[rune pouch]], or [[max cape]]/[[Construct. cape|construction cape]], and use an [[ornate rejuvenation pool]]. **Players may teleport to [[Lunar Isle]] through a portal and bank with [['Birds-Eye' Jack]], then talk to any other person to get kicked off back to Rellekka, or by using the [[Return Orb|Return orb]] next to the bank booth **Players may also attune a POH Portal or Teleport Nexus to Waterbirth Island, and quickly take the boat back to Rellekka, which leaves you at the west dock, only a few steps away from the Vorkath dock. *'''POH & Camelot teleport:''' After completing the [[Hard Kandarin Diary]], you may switch Camelot teleport to the location of the [[Seers' Village bank]]. While having dust runes, chaos runes, and law runes in a rune pouch, Teleport to Seers' Village to bank and teleport back to POH while having your house in Rellekka. *'''Ferox Enclave''': Teleport to [[Ferox Enclave]] with a [[ring of dueling]], use the Pool of Refreshment to reset your stats and use the bank chest to re-gear. Then, return to Vorkath using Fremennik sea boots 4, or teleporting to POH located in Rellekka. '''Note:''' this does not restore special attack, and resets any stats (boosted or drained) upon exiting. **Ferox Enclave also allows you to consume [[blighted anglerfish]] within to boost health above maximum (particularly after consuming a divine potion) for considerably cheaper than using [[anglerfish]] for the same purpose, and not risking entering the [[wilderness]]. *'''Nardah:''' Teleport to [[Nardah]] with your [[Desert amulet 4|Desert Amulet 4]] and pray at the [[Elidinis Statuette]] there, which provides the same benefits as an Ornate rejuvenation pool (excluding stat restoration), as well as a temporary boost to hit points above the player's maximum based on level. A bank is located nearby to the south. This method requires completion of the elite [[Desert Diary]]. ==Equipment== Ranged= The [[dragon hunter crossbow]], (with [[ruby dragon bolts (e)|ruby]] and [[diamond dragon bolts (e)]]), is the most effective ranged weapon to use against [[Vorkath]]. The crossbow's [[passive effect]] damage and accuracy boosts stack with the [[Salve amulet (i)]] / [[Salve amulet(ei)]] and [[Void Knight equipment]]. When using diamond dragon bolts for the last third of the kill instead of a blowpipe, Elite Void generally has better damage output than Masori armour. However, Masori provides additional magic defence over Elite Void, which can extend trips by reducing the amount of [[food]] eaten – especially relevant if using a [[reagent pouch]] to store crushed superior dragon bones.

The [[Toxic blowpipe]] (especially with defence reductions), with Masori armour can provide higher damage output than the dragon hunter crossbow as the ruby bolts' damage begins to fall off. However, the blowpipe is much more expensive to use over time (especially with [[dragon dart|dragon darts]]), and requires praying magic without a shield which significantly increases overall damage received from ranged and dragonfire attacks.

Players should note that the [[Salve amulet]] and [[Salve amulet (e)]] provide no bonuses to Ranged whatsoever; the amulet must be imbued (i) or (ei) to provide [[Ranged bonuses]]. Rings of recoil do not inflict damage on Vorkath.

In order to take the least amount of damage possible, players should use a [[dragonfire ward]] or [[anti-dragon shield]] with a crossbow, while praying [[Protect from Missiles]]. The [[dragonfire shield]] is an inferior option to the anti-dragon shield despite its defences, as it provides a negative Ranged bonus. Other offhand items such as the [[twisted buckler]] or [[book of law]] force players to pray [[Protect from Magic]], causing them to take more damage each kill, and shortening their trips.

With 99 Ranged and a high [[Defence]] level, players should expect anywhere from 3-6 kills with this setup. {{Recommended equipment |style = Ranged (crossbow) |head1 = {{plink|Masori mask (f)}} / {{plink|Void ranger helm}}{{efn|Only use with the full set of Void knight armour.|name=void}} |head2 = {{plink|Masori mask}} / {{plink|Serpentine helm}}{{efn|Negates the need to bring [[anti-venom]] potions|name=serp}} |head3 = {{plink|Crystal helm}} |head4 = {{plink|Karil's coif}} / {{plink|Hueycoatl hide coif}}
/ {{plink|Blessed coif|pic=Ancient coif}} |head5 = {{plink|Archer helm}} |neck1 = {{plink|Salve amulet(ei)}} |cape1 = {{plink|Dizana's quiver}} |cape2 = {{plink|Ava's assembler}} |cape3 = {{plink|Ava's accumulator}} / {{plink|Ranging cape}} |body1 = {{plink|Masori body (f)}} / {{plink|Elite void top}}{{efn|name=void}} |body2 = {{plink|Masori body}} / {{plink|Void knight top}}{{efn|name=void}} |body3 = {{plink|Crystal body}} |body4 = {{plink|Karil's leathertop}} / {{plink|Hueycoatl hide body}}
/ {{plink|Blessed body|pic=Ancient d'hide body}} |body5 = {{plink|Black d'hide body}} |legs1 = {{plink|Masori chaps (f)}} / {{plink|Elite void robe}}{{efn|name=void}} |legs2 = {{plink|Masori chaps}} / {{plink|Void knight robe}}{{efn|name=void}} |legs3 = {{plink|Crystal legs}} |legs4 = {{plink|Karil's leatherskirt}} / {{plink|Hueycoatl hide chaps}}
/ {{plink|Blessed chaps|pic=Ancient chaps}} |legs5 = {{plink|Black d'hide chaps}} |weapon1 = {{plink|Dragon hunter crossbow}} |weapon2 = {{plink|Toxic blowpipe}}{{efn|name=dragonfire}} |weapon3 = {{plink|Bow of faerdhinen}}{{efn|Only with crystal armour.}}{{efn|Loses some anti-fire protection. You must pray [[protect from magic]].|name=dragonfire}}{{efn|name=acid}} |weapon4 = {{plink|Armadyl crossbow}}{{efn|Cannot safely attack during the acid phase|name=acid}} /
{{plink|Dragon crossbow}} |weapon5 = {{plink|Rune crossbow}} |shield1 = {{plink|Dragonfire ward}} / {{plink|Twisted buckler}}{{efn|name=dragonfire}} |shield2 = {{plink|Anti-dragon shield}} |ammo1 = {{plink|Ruby dragon bolts (e)}}{{efn|Use until 250-300 HP (33-40%).|name=ruby}} +
{{plink|Diamond dragon bolts (e)}} |ammo2 = {{plink|Ruby bolts (e)}}{{efn|name=ruby}} +
{{plink|Diamond bolts (e)}} |hands1 = {{plink|Zaryte vambraces}} / {{plink|Void knight gloves}}{{efn|name=void}} |hands2 = {{plink|Barrows gloves}} |feet1 = {{plink|Avernic treads (max)}} |feet2 = {{plink|Pegasian boots}} |feet3 = {{plink|Ranger boots}} |feet4 = {{plink|Blessed boots|pic=Ancient d'hide boots}} /
{{plink|Aranea boots}} |feet5 = {{plink|Mixed hide boots}} |ring1 = {{plink|Venator ring}} / {{plink|Lightbearer}} |ring2 = {{plink|Archers ring (i)}} |ring3 = {{plink|Ring of suffering (ri)}}{{efn|Used only for defence bonuses, as Vorkath is immune to recoil}} |ring4 = {{plink|Ring of shadows}} |ring5 = {{plink|Explorer's ring 4|txt=Explorer ring}} |special1 = {{plink|Zaryte crossbow}} |special2 = {{plink|Elder maul}} / {{plink|Tonalztics of ralos}} |special3 = {{plink|Bandos godsword}} / {{plink|Dragon warhammer}} |special4 = {{plink|Armadyl crossbow}} |special5 = {{plink|Toxic blowpipe}} }} {{notelist}} |-| Melee= The [[dragon hunter lance]] is the most effective melee weapon to use against [[Vorkath]]. The lance's [[passive effect]] damage and accuracy boosts stack with the [[Salve amulet (e)]] or [[Salve amulet(ei)]], allowing players to hit frequently and high. Players use [[Protect from Magic]] and wear the best [[melee armour]] possible to maximise their ranged defence. Players should stay in melee range when not dealing with specials, as despite taking the occasional melee, being out of melee range increases the rate of ranged attacks and results in more damage taken overall. If using defence-draining specials, the [[Bandos godsword]] is superior over the [[dragon warhammer]] due to the increased accuracy even when factoring in a [[defender]] for the warhammer. '''If your gear has -64 magic attack bonus or lower, your [[Crumble Undead]] will not hit.'''

Below Bandos equipment, prioritise defensive bonuses over strength bonus if you find your trips cut short by Vorkath's normal melee and ranged attacks. With regard to profit, consistently higher kills per trip are more valuable than marginal damage gains. Additionally, if sacrificing defensive bonuses for melee strength in a given slot, ensure that you are actually gaining a max hit (check with the [https://tools.runescape.wiki/osrs-dps/ DPS Calculator]) and that you are not reducing your kills per trip. For example, depending on your other equips and Strength level, it is possible that the +2 melee strength bonus of [[Bandos tassets]] does not improve your max hit and instead increases the likelihood that your trips are cut short compared to using [[Justiciar legguards]], which has higher melee and ranged defences. {{Recommended equipment |style = Melee |head1 = {{plink|Torva full helm}} /
{{plink|Slayer helmet (i)}}{{efn|name=slayer}} |head2 = {{plink|Neitiznot faceguard}} /
{{plink|Serpentine helm}}{{efn|Negates the need to bring [[anti-venom]] potions|name=serp}} / {{plink|Oathplate helm}} |head3 = {{plink|Justiciar faceguard}}{{efn|With full [[Justiciar armour]]|name=justihelm}} / {{plink|Blood moon helm}} |head4 = {{plink|Helm of neitiznot}} / {{plink|Barrows helm|pic=Torag's helm}} |neck1 = {{plink|Amulet of rancour}} / {{plink|Salve amulet (e)}}{{efn|DPS is slightly higher in max melee gear with slayer helm (i) and amulet of rancour or torture compared to salve amulet (ei) with torva full helm, but can vary with your individual setup. Only on a [[blue dragon]], [[zombie]], or Vorkath slayer task.|name=slayer}} |neck2 = {{plink|Amulet of torture}}{{efn|name=slayer}} |cape1 = {{plink|Infernal cape}} |cape2 = {{plink|Fire cape}} |cape3 = {{plink|Ardougne cloak 4|txt=Ardougne cloak}} |body1 = {{plink|Torva platebody}} |body2 = {{plink|Bandos chestplate}} / {{plink|Oathplate chest}} |body3 = {{plink|Justiciar chestguard}} / {{plink|Blood moon chestplate}} |body4 = {{plink|Barrows platebody|pic=Torag's platebody}} / {{plink|Fighter torso}} |legs1 = {{plink|Torva platelegs}} |legs2 = {{plink|Bandos tassets}} / {{plink|Oathplate legs}} |legs3 = {{plink|Justiciar legguards}} / {{plink|Blood moon tassets}} |legs4 = {{plink|Barrows platelegs|pic=Torag's platelegs}} |weapon1 = {{plink|Dragon hunter lance}} |weapon2 = {{plink|Osmumten's fang}} |weapon3 = {{plink|Ghrazi rapier}} |weapon4 = {{plink|Zamorakian hasta}} |shield1 = {{plink|Avernic defender}} |shield2 = {{plink|Dragonfire shield}} / {{plink|Dragon defender}} |ammo1 = {{plink|Rada's blessing 4}} |ammo2 = {{plink|God blessing|pic=Peaceful blessing}} / {{plink|Rada's blessing 3}} / {{plink|Rada's blessing 2}} |hands1 = {{plink|Ferocious gloves}} |hands2 = {{plink|Barrows gloves}} |feet1 = {{plink|Avernic treads (max)}} |feet2 = {{plink|Primordial boots}} / {{plink|Echo boots}}{{efn|Used only for defence bonuses, as Vorkath is immune to recoil}} |feet3 = {{plink|Dragon boots}} / {{plink|Guardian boots}} |feet4 = {{plink|Granite boots}} / {{plink|Rune boots}} |ring1 = {{plink|Ultor ring}} |ring2 = {{plink|Berserker ring (i)}} /
{{plink|Lightbearer}} |special1 = {{plink|Burning claws}} (on stab){{efn|Use at the start of the fight, or after a Rapid Fire/Zombified Spawn special attack from Vorkath as burn damage is nullified during these special attacks.|name=bclaws}}/{{plink|Voidwaker}} |special2 = {{plink|Dragon claws}} /
{{plink|Elder maul}} |special3 = {{plink|Bandos godsword}} >
{{plink|Dragon warhammer}} |special4 = {{plink|Bone dagger}} }} {{notelist}} ==Inventory setups== In all setups, [[chaos rune]]s must be brought in order to cast [[Crumble Undead]], along with [[dust rune]]s (or [[Air rune|air]] and [[earth rune]]s). A [[rune pouch]] (or its [[Divine rune pouch|divine variant]]) can be used to significantly reduce the number of inventory slots needed. As air and earth runes will already be brought, [[law rune]]s can be brought to cast [[Teleport to House]] as a cheap teleport option. [[Fire rune|Fire]] and [[nature rune]]s can also be brought to cast [[High Level Alchemy]] on Vorkath's valuable drops, but will take up additional inventory space. Much of the value of Vorkath's drops is in its guaranteed drop of [[superior dragon bones]]. To save space in inventory without a significant loss in profit, if not all bones can be picked up at once, a [[pestle and mortar]] and [[reagent pouch]] can be brought to convert the bones to [[crushed superior dragon bones]], which can be stored in the pouch. Be aware that crushed bones will not sell as quickly as the original version. A [[Huntsman's kit]] with multiple [[Imp-in-a-box]] stored inside can also be used to effectively increase the number of drops that can be picked up. Ranged (crossbow)= {{Equipment|align = right |buttons = No |head = Void ranger helm |cape = Dizana's quiver |neck = Salve amulet(ei) |ammo = Ruby dragon bolts (e) |weapon = Dragon hunter crossbow |torso = Elite void top |legs = Elite void robe |shield = Dragonfire ward |gloves = Void knight gloves |boots = Avernic treads (max) |ring = Lightbearer }} {{Inventory |align = right |1 = Diamond dragon bolts (e) |2 = Divine ranging potion(3) |3 = Extended super antifire(2) |4 = Extended anti-venom+ |5 = Zaryte crossbow |6 = {{Cheap prayer}} |7 = {{Cheap prayer}} |8 = {{Cheap prayer}} |9 = Cooked karambwan |10 = {{Cheap food}} |11 = {{Cheap food}} |12 = {{Cheap food}} |13 = {{Cheap food}} |14 = {{Cheap food}} |15 = Cooked karambwan |16 = Cooked karambwan |17 = Cooked karambwan |18 = Cooked karambwan |19 = Cooked karambwan |20 = Cooked karambwan |21 = Cooked karambwan |22 = Cooked karambwan |23 = Cooked karambwan |24 = Cooked karambwan |25 = Divine rune pouch |26 = Guthix rest |27 = Guthix rest |28 = Guthix rest }} {{Rune pouch|align=right |1=Law rune |2=Dust rune |3=Chaos rune }} *An ammo switch (if using crossbow) *[[Zaryte crossbow]] preferably. [[Toxic blowpipe]] alternatively (for [[special]], if using crossbow) *1 [[extended anti-venom+]] (skip if using [[Serpentine helm]]) *3-4 [[super restore]]s or [[prayer potions]] (crossbow only; 1-2 with blowpipe) *[[Rune pouch]] with [[dust rune]]s, [[law runes]], and [[chaos runes]] to cast [[Crumble Undead]] spells and [[Teleport to House]] *[[Divine ranging potion]] or a [[Divine bastion potion]] *[[Extended super antifire]] (regular [[extended antifire]] works, but is less effective) *An emergency [[teleport]] (optional) *A teleport to [[Rellekka]] (see [[#Vorkath trip efficiency|the section above for teleports of choice]]) *[[Food]] ([[sharks]] or better, preferably [[manta ray]]s for longer trips; with very high stats and optimal gear, an inventory of karambwans is likely to be more than enough while they have a lower attack delay when eaten during combat) *[[Guthix rest]] allow you to heal small amounts of health without losing ticks, otherwise [[cooked karambwan]] are useful for combo eating at low HP. It can also be used as a replacement to extended anti-venom+, as it reduces venom to poison. '''Tip''': Players may use their antifire and stat boost potions at a bank to create more room for food. '''Tip:''' Bringing a [[Slayer's staff]] while setting Crumble Undead as the auto-cast spell is an easy transition and safer alternative to using the spell from the spellbook. {{clear}} |-| Ranged (blowpipe)= {{Equipment|align = right |buttons = No |head = Ancient coif |cape = Ava's assembler |neck = Salve amulet(ei) |ammo = Rada's blessing 4 |weapon = Toxic blowpipe |torso = Ancient d'hide body |legs = Ancient chaps |shield = |gloves = Barrows gloves |boots = Ancient d'hide boots |ring = Lightbearer }} {{Inventory | align = right | 1 = Bandos godsword | 2 = Dragon boots | 3 = Extended super antifire(4) | 4 = Extended anti-venom+(4) | 5 = Fire cape | 6 = Berserker ring (i) | 7 = Divine ranging potion(4) | 8 = {{Cheap prayer}} | 9 = {{Cheap food}} | 10 = {{Cheap food}} | 11 = {{Cheap prayer}} | 12 = {{Cheap prayer}} | 13 = Cooked karambwan | 14 = Cooked karambwan | 15 = Cooked karambwan | 16 = Cooked karambwan | 17 = {{Cheap food}} | 18 = {{Cheap food}} | 19 = {{Cheap food}} | 20 = {{Cheap food}} | 21 = {{Cheap food}} | 22 = {{Cheap food}} | 23 = {{Cheap food}} | 24 = {{Cheap food}} | 25 = Super combat potion(4) | 26 = {{Cheap food}} | 27 = {{Cheap food}} | 28 = Rune pouch }} {{Rune pouch | align = right | 1 = Law rune | 2 = Dust rune | 3 = Chaos rune }} '''Note:''' This setup is only recommended if you do not have a dragon hunter crossbow or dragon hunter lance. *Melee gear for 2 special attacks. will almost never splash (Alternatives are [[slayer's staff (e)]], [[staff of the dead]], [[toxic staff of the dead]], [[staff of light]], [[staff of balance]], [[void knight mace]], [[Skull sceptre]].) *[[Voidwaker]] or [[Burning claws]] for special attacks *1 [[extended anti-venom+]] (skip if using [[Serpentine helm]]) *3-4 [[super restore]] or [[prayer potion]] *[[Rune pouch]] with [[dust rune]]s, [[law runes]], and [[chaos runes]] to cast [[Crumble Undead]] spells and [[Teleport to House]] *[[Super combat potion]] or [[divine super combat potion]] *[[Extended super antifire]] (Players may find it worthwhile to bring more to overheal before each kill) *[[Food]] ([[sharks]] or better, preferably [[manta ray]]s or [[dark crab]]s to ensure longer trips) '''Tip''': Players may use their antifire and stat boost potions at a bank to create more room for food. '''Tip:''' If choosing not to use a staff to autocast [[Crumble Undead]], make sure that your magic bonus is higher than -64, otherwise you will occasionally splash on the spawn. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Vorkath}} [[Category:Strategies]]
